steps.text: Install the CEMS at an accessible location downstream of all pollution control equipment where the metals concentrations measurements are directly representative or can be corrected so as to be representative of the total emissions from the affected facility.
Select representative measurement points or paths.
Install the CEMS and prepare the RM test site according to the specifications in Section 3.
Prepare the CEMS for operation according to the manufacturer’s written instructions.
Determine the zero drift period while the affected facility is operating at more than 50 percent of normal load.
Conduct the CD test once each day for 7 consecutive days.
Conduct a RA test following the CD test period while the affected facility is operating at more than 50 percent of normal load.
Conduct the CD and ZD tests at the points specified in Sections 4.3 and 4.4.
Record the CEMS response and calculate the CD according to the formula provided.
Calculate the ZD according to the provided formula.

preconditions.text: Ensure no unscheduled maintenance, repair, or manual adjustments of the CEMS took place during the 24-hour period.

steps.text: Conduct the RM measurements in such a way that they will yield results representative of the emissions from the source and can be correlated to the CEMS data.
Conduct the diluent and moisture measurements that are taken within a 30- to 60-minute period, which includes the pollutant measurements, to calculate dry pollutant concentration.
Determine the concentration of each metal for compliance purposes by the CEMS.
Determine the RA at three levels (0 to 20, 40 to 60, and 80 to 120 percent of the emission limit) for one of the metals which will be monitored.
Note the beginning and end of each RM test period of each run (including the exact time of day) in the CEMS data log.
Correlate the CEMS and RM test data as to the time and duration by determining the integrated average pollutant concentration for each RM test period.
Compare each integrated CEMS value against the corresponding average RM value.
Obtain a minimum of three pairs of CEMS and RM measurements.
Calculate the arithmetic mean of a data set as follows:
Calculate the arithmetic mean of the difference, d, of a data set, using Equation 3 and substituting d for x.
Calculate the standard deviation (SD) of a data set as follows:
Calculate the RA as follows:
Summarize in tabular form the results of the CD tests and the RA tests or alternate RA procedure as appropriate.
Include all data sheets, calculations, and records of CEMS response necessary to substantiate that the performance of the CEMS met the performance specifications.
Report the CEMS measurements to the agency in units of µg/m on a dry basis, corrected to 20°C or 7 percent O2.
Develop a procedure for a total system calibration when applicable, for determining RA.
